title: Algorithm Analysis Report
summary: Analyze the time and space complexity of selected course implementations and compare design tradeoffs.
studentInstructions: Analyze at least six operations or functions from your previous assignments, including at least two linked structure operations and two tree operations.
For each selected operation, state the time complexity, space complexity, and the code behavior that supports your claim.
Include one comparison between two possible implementations, such as linked-list stack versus Python list stack or breadth-first search using a queue versus recursive depth-first traversal.
Discuss at least two tradeoffs involving speed, memory, simplicity, or maintainability.

rubric.description: The report gives accurate Big-O time complexity for at least six selected operations or functions.
The report gives accurate space complexity and accounts for auxiliary structures or recursive call stack usage.
Each claim is supported by references to loops, recursion patterns, traversal behavior, or data structure operations.
The report compares two plausible implementations and explains how their complexity or behavior differs.
The report discusses at least two meaningful tradeoffs involving performance, memory, simplicity, or maintainability.
The report is readable, structured, and uses terminology consistently.

gradingInstructions: Use only this assignment and its rubric.
Check required files before grading quality.
